rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAGILAVY FAMILY TRUST ESTABLISHED JUNE 5, 1989 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioners Notice Of Motion And Motion For Dismissal Of Petition For Reimbursement Of Trustees Attorneys Fees, Interpretation, Appointment Of Receiver And Constructive Trust; And For Monetary Awards
rulings.Rulings: Denied. Although some issues are raised in teh petition that depend on the ultimate outcome of other issues, there is nothing inappropriate about putting them in a petition at this time. Any issues not eventually made moot can be decided by the Court in the proper order. The Court expects Respondent to comply with its February 21, 2017 Order, but Petitioner's request to deduct that amount from money otherwise due is denied. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (414-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, September 5, 2017.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAGILAVY FAMILY TRUST ESTABLISHED JUNE 5, 1989 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings For Petitioners Petition For Orders For Conveyance Or Transfer Of Property Held By Trustee Claimed To Belong To Another (Amended)
rulings.Rulings: Even assuming this is otherwise a proper motion to bring against a probate petition, it is denied because it depends on material facts that have been controverted by respondents' pleadings. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (414-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, September 5, 2017.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAGILAVY FAMILY TRUST ESTABLISHED JUNE 5, 1989 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings For Petition For Interpretation Of Terms Of Trust And For Relief From Breach Of Trust
rulings.Rulings: Even assuming this is otherwise a proper motion to bring against a probate petition, it is denied because it depends on material facts that have been controverted by respondents' pleadings. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (414-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, September 5, 2017.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAGILAVY FAMILY TRUST ESTABLISHED JUNE 5, 1989 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Respondents 3-30-2016 Petition For Orders Determining Ownership Of Trust Property: Directing Third Party To Account For Trust Property: Imposing A Constructive Trust; And Directing Transfer Of Property To Trust; For Summary Adjudication Of The Affirmative Defenses Of Unclean Hands And Unjust Enrichment In Respondents 3-30-2016 Response To Petition For Interpretation Of Terms Of Trust; And For Summary Adjudicat
rulings.Rulings: Denied. Petitioner's separate statement is not in the form required by CRC 3.1350(d)(1) in that it does not track the claims and defenses listed in the notice of motion and the seperate statement addreses discrete issues many of which would not resolve an entire claim or defense, and the motion is denied on that basis. In any event, Petitioner has not carried her initial burden of demonstrating that Respondents cannot prove its claims or defenses, and the motion is denied on that basis. In any event, Respondents have raised triable issues of material fact on several issues necessary to the motion, including at least the existence of an ownership agreement, the validity of signed agreements, and the existence of any fraud, and the motion is denied on this additional basis. To the extent the motion seeks adjudication of claims in Petitioner's November 22, 2016 Petition for Orders, which has since been amended, the motion is also denied as moot. Respondents shall prepare and submit a form of order consisitent with this tentative ruling and compliant with CCP section 437c(g). Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, September 5, 2017.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT LAMAR DUMAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. although the interim order clearly stated the transfer had to be made through a licensed firearm dealer, and the DOJ letter also confirms transfers are handled through dealers only, it appears petitioner just applied for firearm ownership, and did not follow the directions in the order re transfer protocol. If the transfer was made through a FFL as required, the transfer receipt from that FFL must be filed prior to the hearing.
